name: The Olivet discourse
notes: Jesus' prophecy on the mount of Olives, given in all three synoptics. Matthew and Mark run closely in parallel through the whole discourse; Luke shares the opening structure but reworks the middle, replacing the abomination of desolation with Jerusalem compassed with armies and dropping the false-Christs warning and the fig-tree's heavenly signs that Matthew and Mark keep. Matthew's parables of 24:32 onward and much of the closing exhortation are his alone, so links thin out toward the end. Only beats with genuinely shared wording or content are linked; Luke's divergences are left unlinked.
